item.text: The broad sweep found one last stale reference: the checkpoint upload lifecycle still names the old public `PATCH /api/v1/training/jobs/.../steps/...` path instead of the new internal endpoint. I’m confirming the exact line before I finalize the verdict.

item.aggregated_output:
608	**Checkpoint upload lifecycle:**
   609	1. Container writes checkpoint to `/workspace/ipc/checkpoint/{iteration}/` + renames `.done` marker (atomic)
   610	2. Watcher detects `.done` marker → uploads entire dir to MinIO `training-jobs/{job_id}/checkpoints/{iteration}/`
   611	3. Upload succeeds → watcher calls `PATCH /api/v1/training/jobs/{job_id}/steps/{step_id}` with `last_checkpoint_path = "training-jobs/{job_id}/checkpoints/{iteration}/"`
   612	4. Upload fails → watcher retries 3x with exponential backoff. If all retries fail → log error, do NOT update `last_checkpoint_path` (resume will use previous valid checkpoint)
   613	5. New checkpoint arrives while uploading → watcher queues it; after current upload completes, uploads newest only (skip intermediate)

item.text:
### ISSUE-1: Checkpoint lifecycle still references the old public PATCH route
- Category: correctness
- Severity: medium
- Problem: Section 7 now defines the watcher write-back path under `/api/v1/internal/*` ([spec](/Users/sunsilkk/service/ml_management_v2/docs/specs/2026-04-10-ml-training-platform-design.md#L958)), but the checkpoint upload lifecycle still says the watcher calls `PATCH /api/v1/training/jobs/{job_id}/steps/{step_id}` after upload ([spec](/Users/sunsilkk/service/ml_management_v2/docs/specs/2026-04-10-ml-training-platform-design.md#L608)).
- Why it matters: The runtime flow is still not fully aligned with the new internal-endpoint/auth model, so implementers can wire the watcher to the wrong route.
- Suggested fix: Replace that stale public path with the corresponding `/api/v1/internal/jobs/{id}/steps/{step_id}` route and do one final sweep to ensure every watcher example uses the internal endpoint family consistently.

### VERDICT
- Status: REVISE
- Reason: The spec is nearly there, but one stale watcher endpoint reference still conflicts with the internal API contract.
